domain: Reinforcement Learning for SLM Reasoning (GRPO/RLVR Tools)

gaps.title: Reward Function Marketplace / Reusable Reward Library
gaps.description: No centralized, plug-and-play library of verified reward functions for different reasoning domains. Every team writes reward functions from scratch for math, code, logic, science, etc. The Verifiers library exists but covers only basic patterns. No sharing/discovery mechanism for community-built reward functions.
evidence: Each framework (TRL, OpenRLHF, veRL) has its own reward function format with no interoperability
Label Studio templates are the closest thing to reusable rewards but are annotation-focused, not training-focused
RLVR research shows reward design is the #1 challenge — yet no tool addresses this systematically
Reward hacking is a critical problem, and verified/tested reward functions could mitigate it
gaps.affected_users: ML engineers, researchers, startups building reasoning models

gaps.title: Curriculum / Difficulty Scheduling for RL Training
gaps.description: No tool provides automated curriculum learning for GRPO/RLVR training. Research (E2H Reasoner, VCRL) shows easy-to-hard scheduling dramatically improves sample efficiency and final performance, but no framework implements this as a built-in feature. Users must manually curate difficulty-sorted datasets.
evidence: E2H Reasoner paper shows curriculum RL requires fewer total samples than direct RL
VCRL uses reward variance as difficulty proxy — implementable but not in any tool
Scaf-GRPO provides scaffolding but only at a coarse level, not fine-grained curriculum
No framework offers automatic difficulty estimation for training examples
gaps.affected_users: Anyone training reasoning models, especially with limited compute

gaps.title: One-Click GRPO Training for Non-ML Engineers
gaps.description: Only Unsloth Studio offers a no-code UI, and it's limited to their ecosystem. No tool lets a product engineer or domain expert train a reasoning model without writing Python. The barrier to entry for GRPO/RLVR is still very high despite the algorithms being relatively simple.
evidence: Unsloth Studio launched March 2026 but is single-vendor and single-GPU only
TRL, OpenRLHF, veRL all require significant Python/ML expertise
HuggingFace AutoTrain doesn't support GRPO/RLVR
Growing demand from domain experts (legal, medical, finance) who want custom reasoning models
gaps.affected_users: Domain experts, product engineers, non-ML teams at startups

gaps.title: RL Training Observability / Debugging Dashboard
gaps.description: No dedicated tool for monitoring, debugging, and diagnosing RL training runs for reasoning models. Users rely on TensorBoard/W&B for basic loss curves but lack reward distribution analysis, reward hacking detection, response quality tracking, policy drift monitoring, and training stability diagnostics.
evidence: Reward hacking is documented as a major problem but detection tools don't exist
GRPO-specific metrics (group reward variance, advantage estimation quality) not tracked by any dashboard
Homogeneous response problem (all correct or all incorrect in a group) causes null gradients — no tool flags this
Training instability with long CoT is common but difficult to diagnose
gaps.affected_users: ML engineers running RL training jobs

gaps.title: Cross-Domain Verifiable Reward Engine (Beyond Math/Code)
gaps.description: RLVR has proven effective for math and code where verification is straightforward (string matching, code execution). But domains like science, medicine, law, and business reasoning lack standardized verification tools. No framework provides pluggable verifiers for these domains.
evidence: Research extends RLVR to medical QA, psychology, dialogue but with custom one-off verifiers
K2V framework extends RLVR to knowledge-intensive domains but remains a paper
No tool provides domain-specific verification templates (e.g., legal citation checking, medical protocol adherence)
Guru dataset covers 6 domains but the verification infrastructure is minimal
gaps.affected_users: Domain-specific model builders (healthcare, legal, finance, science)

gaps.title: Unified Small-Model RL Training Platform
gaps.description: Small models (1B-7B) are the sweet spot for edge/on-device reasoning, but no platform optimizes the full RL pipeline for them. Unsloth optimizes VRAM but lacks algorithm breadth. DeepScaleR/SimpleRL are research code. Enterprise tools (NeMo-RL, ROLL) optimize for scale, not efficiency on consumer hardware.
evidence: Unsloth: great VRAM efficiency but only GRPO, single-GPU (open source), no PPO/DAPO/REINFORCE++
Open-RS (AAAI 2026) studies RL for small LLMs but produces no tool
DeepScaleR achieves SOTA at 1.5B but codebase is research-quality
No tool provides small-model-specific hyperparameter presets or training recipes
gaps.affected_users: Edge AI companies, mobile ML engineers, resource-constrained researchers

gaps.title: RL Training Cost Estimator and Resource Planner
gaps.description: No tool estimates the compute cost, time, or GPU requirements for an RL training run before starting. Users guess at batch sizes, group sizes, generation lengths, and number of epochs with no guidance. This leads to wasted GPU hours and failed runs.
evidence: RL training spends 80% of compute on generation — generation parameters dominate cost
GRPO group size, number of completions per prompt, and max generation length all interact unpredictably
Predictive scaling laws for GRPO exist (July 2025 paper) but no tool implements them
Cloud GPU costs make trial-and-error expensive for small teams
existing_partial_solutions: Scaling laws paper (not implemented in any tool)

gaps.title: Multi-Domain / Multi-Task RL Training Orchestrator
gaps.description: Training a model to reason across multiple domains (math + code + logic + science) requires manual mixing of datasets and reward functions. No tool provides intelligent multi-task RL training with automatic balancing, curriculum mixing, and domain-specific reward routing.
evidence: DeepSeek R1 trains on math, code, and logic simultaneously but their pipeline is proprietary
Guru dataset spans 6 domains but no tool leverages it for multi-domain training
Task interference in multi-domain RL is well-documented but no tool mitigates it
Domain experts want to add their domain to an existing reasoning model without full retraining

gaps.title: RL Training Data Curation and Quality Pipeline
gaps.description: Curating high-quality training prompts with verifiable answers is a major bottleneck. No tool helps users discover, filter, generate, or validate RL training data for reasoning tasks. Users scrape datasets manually or use a handful of known benchmarks.
evidence: SimpleRL shows 8K examples suffice for significant gains — but finding the RIGHT 8K is hard
Data quality and difficulty distribution dramatically impact RL outcomes
No tool for generating synthetic training prompts with verified solutions
No tool for estimating example difficulty before training
gaps.affected_users: Anyone starting GRPO/RLVR training from scratch

gaps.title: Automated Reward Hacking Detection and Mitigation
gaps.description: Reward hacking (models exploiting reward function flaws) is the biggest reliability issue in RL training. No tool automatically detects reward hacking patterns: unnecessary reasoning steps, correct answers via wrong logic, format gaming, or length exploitation.
evidence: Research documents multiple reward hacking patterns in LLM RL training
Dr-GRPO and DAPO address specific biases (length, difficulty) but not general reward hacking
PRM Clip and Delta mechanisms exist in papers but not in production tools
Manual inspection of model outputs is the current primary detection method

gaps.title: RL-Specific Model Evaluation Suite for Reasoning
gaps.description: Evaluating RL-trained reasoning models uses the same benchmarks as SFT models (AIME, MATH, GSM8K). No tool measures RL-specific qualities: reasoning chain quality, search efficiency improvement, generalization across difficulty levels, or capability expansion vs. search compression.
evidence: Research debate: RLVR may improve search efficiency rather than reasoning capability
Current benchmarks fail to distinguish models that genuinely learned vs. overfit
No tool measures pass@k to pass@1 efficiency improvement separately from capability expansion
Counterfactual evaluation and difficulty-stratified testing not supported by any eval tool
gaps.affected_users: Researchers, teams needing to validate RL training actually improved reasoning

gaps.title: Framework Interoperability / Model Portability Layer
gaps.description: Models and training configurations are not portable across RL frameworks. A model fine-tuned with TRL cannot easily continue training on OpenRLHF. Reward functions written for one framework don't work on another. No standard format exists for RL training recipes.
evidence: Each framework has its own reward function API, config format, and checkpoint format
Teams often switch frameworks mid-project as requirements change
HuggingFace model format provides base portability but RL-specific state (optimizer, training stats) is lost
No standard for describing RL training recipes that works across frameworks
gaps.affected_users: Teams evaluating or switching between frameworks

gaps.title: Process Reward Model (PRM) Training and Serving Toolkit
gaps.description: Outcome-based rewards (correct/incorrect) are limited. Process reward models that score intermediate reasoning steps are far more powerful but extremely difficult to train. No accessible tool helps train, evaluate, and serve PRMs for use in RL training loops.
evidence: RM-R1 proposes chain-of-rubrics for reward modeling but remains a paper
PRM training requires step-level annotations which are expensive to create
TRL has RewardTrainer but it's outcome-level, not process-level
Process rewards significantly reduce reward hacking and improve reasoning quality
gaps.affected_users: Advanced RL practitioners seeking better-than-binary rewards

gaps.title: RL Training for Agentic Reasoning (Tool Use + Reasoning)
gaps.description: Combining reasoning RL with tool-use/agentic capabilities is the next frontier but poorly tooled. Only OpenRLHF, ROLL, and AReaL have early agentic RL support. No tool provides a unified framework for training models that reason AND use tools/APIs in a verified manner.
evidence: OpenRLHF async agent RLHF is new (0.8.0) and limited
ROLL ROME introduces agentic model training but is Alibaba-ecosystem-specific
NeMo-Gym aims at RL environments but lacks agentic reasoning focus
Growing demand as reasoning models are deployed in production with tool access

gaps.title: Consumer-Priced Cloud RL Training Service
gaps.description: No managed service offers affordable GRPO/RLVR training. Cloud GPU costs are high, RL training is compute-intensive, and setting up distributed training is complex. A service offering 'train your reasoning model for $X/hour' at consumer-accessible prices doesn't exist.
evidence: RL training spends 80% of compute on generation, making it 3-5x more expensive than SFT
Together AI, Fireworks, etc. offer fine-tuning APIs but not GRPO/RLVR
Unsloth targets single-GPU users but cloud deployment still requires infrastructure expertise
Academic researchers often can't afford multi-GPU RL training runs
gaps.affected_users: Individual developers, small startups, academic researchers
